item.title: Building a Compact Handwritten OCR Pipeline
item.description:
Introduction Link to heading

A while back, the Handwritten to Data Kaggle competition focused on Ukrainian handwriting recognition. The dataset is publicly available on Hugging Face as UkrainianCatholicUniversity/rukopys.

Part of my day job is on the deployment side of ML — porting and optimizing models to run on mobile phones, where the recurring questions are “does this fit in memory?”, “does it hit the latency budget?”, and “does it run on the target hardware?”. Within that, I work mostly on document AI: page detection, document classification, image enhancement. Handwritten-text recognition, though, was a piece of the stack I had never built end-to-end myself. I treated this dataset as a chance to fill that gap while staying inside the constraints I optimize for at work.

item.title: About
item.description:
Hi, I’m Andrii.

I am an engineer who trains, integrates, and deploys machine-learning models on mobile devices. My work sits between model development and product constraints: accuracy matters, but so do latency, memory use, power consumption, model size, and the reliability of the surrounding pipeline.

This blog documents that work in public. I write about practical ML systems, computer vision, OCR, and the low-level details that determine whether a model is actually usable outside a notebook.
